sentences.quote: We formalize, in the Lean 4 proof assistant, a structural bridge between two long-standing puzzles of the Standard Model: the smallness of the QCD topological angle $|\theta| \lesssim 10^{-9}$ implied by neutron electric-dipole-moment experiments~\cite{Pendlebury2015}, and the observed cosmological-constant energy density $\rhoDE \approx 2.8\times 10^{-11}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$~\cite{Planck2018,DESI2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize, in the lean 4 proof assistant, a structural bridge between two long-standing puzzles of the standard model: the smallness of the qcd topological angle $|\theta| \lesssim 10^{-9}$ implied by neutron electric-dipole-moment experiments~, and the observed cosmological-constant energy density $\rhode \approx 2.8\times 10^{-11}\,ev^4$~.
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim with embedded citations. Pendlebury2015, Planck2018, DESI2024 all in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The bridge is the recent topological dark-energy proposal of Van Waerbeke and Zhitnitsky~\cite{VanWaerbeke2025}, in which the cosmological-constant scale is sourced by the QCD topological vacuum and its predicted value, $\rhoDE \sim \Lqcd^6 / \MP^2$, contains \emph{no free parameters}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bridge is the recent topological dark-energy proposal of van waerbeke and zhitnitsky~, in which the cosmological-constant scale is sourced by the qcd topological vacuum and its predicted value, $\rhode \sim \lqcd^6 / \mp^2$, contains no free parameters.
sentences.agent_notes: VanWaerbeke2025 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: At the Particle-Data-Group QCD scale $\Lqcd = 0.1\,\mathrm{GeV}$ and Planck mass $\MP = 1.22 \times 10^{19}\,\mathrm{GeV}$, the prediction is $\rhoDE \approx 6.7 \times 10^{-9}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$, sitting within roughly $240\times$ of the observed value --- well within the three-orders-of-magnitude window quoted by the Zhitnitsky proposal, and approximately $120$ orders of magnitude below the Planck-natural estimate $\MP^4 \approx 10^{112}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at the particle-data-group qcd scale $\lqcd = 0.1\,gev$ and planck mass $\mp = 1.22 \times 10^{19}\,gev$, the prediction is $\rhode \approx 6.7 \times 10^{-9}\,ev^4$, sitting within roughly $240\times$ of the observed value --- well within the three-orders-of-magnitude window quoted by the zhitnitsky proposal, and approximately $120$ orders of magnitude below the planck-natural estimate $\mp^4 \approx 10^{112}\,ev^4$.
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute: 0.1^6 * 6.71e-3 = 6.71e-9 eV^4; ratio to 2.8e-11 = 239.6 ~ 240. Matches paper to <0.5%.

sentences.quote: We encode the $\theta$-vacuum as a Lean structure carrying the experimental neutron-EDM constraint as a load-bearing invariant, prove that the Planck-natural value $\theta = 1$ violates this invariant by nine orders of magnitude, and derive a structural inconsistency theorem stating that \emph{both} the Zhitnitsky topological mechanism \emph{and} a residual Klinkhamer--Volovik q-theory contribution~\cite{KlinkhamerVolovik2010} cannot be simultaneously active without exceeding the observed dark-energy density by more than a factor of three.
sentences.quote_normalized: we encode the $\theta$-vacuum as a lean structure carrying the experimental neutron-edm constraint as a load-bearing invariant, prove that the planck-natural value $\theta = 1$ violates this invariant by nine orders of magnitude, and derive a structural inconsistency theorem stating that both the zhitnitsky topological mechanism and a residual klinkhamer--volovik q-theory contribution~ cannot be simultaneously active without exceeding the observed dark-energy density by more than a factor of three.
sentences.agent_notes: KlinkhamerVolovik2010 in CITATION_REGISTRY. Refers to formally-verified theorems (theta_planck_natural_violates_edm_bound, combined_zhitnitsky_qtheory_exceeds_observation) — both exist.

sentences.quote: The formalization carries zero \texttt{sorry} statements and introduces no new axioms beyond the Lean kernel's three.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization carries zero sorry statements and introduces no new axioms beyond the lean kernel's three.
sentences.agent_notes: IA recheck: grep '^  sorry' StrongCPTopologicalDE.lean = 0; grep '^axiom ' = 0. Matches.

sentences.quote: The strong-CP problem and the cosmological-constant problem have historically been treated as independent fine-tuning puzzles of fundamental physics.
sentences.quote_normalized: the strong-cp problem and the cosmological-constant problem have historically been treated as independent fine-tuning puzzles of fundamental physics.
sentences.agent_notes: Historical-context qualitative assertion.

sentences.quote: The strong-CP problem asks why the QCD vacuum angle $\theta$ --- a parameter generically expected to be of order unity --- is bounded by neutron-EDM experiments to $|\theta| \lesssim 10^{-9}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the strong-cp problem asks why the qcd vacuum angle $\theta$ --- a parameter generically expected to be of order unity --- is bounded by neutron-edm experiments to $|\theta| \lesssim 10^{-9}$.
sentences.agent_notes: θ < 10^{-9} consistent with Pendlebury2015 (their bound is |d_n| < 3e-26 e cm; θ̄ from this gives |θ̄| ≲ few × 10^{-10}, paper conservatively states 10^{-9}).

sentences.quote: The cosmological-constant problem asks why the observed dark-energy density is $\sim 120$ orders of magnitude below the Planck-natural estimate.
sentences.quote_normalized: the cosmological-constant problem asks why the observed dark-energy density is $\sim 120$ orders of magnitude below the planck-natural estimate.
sentences.agent_notes: 120-order hierarchy is the standard CC-problem framing; reproduced in Lean theorem zhitnitsky_DE_far_below_planck.

sentences.quote: Klinkhamer and Volovik~\cite{KlinkhamerVolovik2010} proposed a q-theory equilibrium-relaxation mechanism in which the cosmological-constant scale is dynamically driven to the QCD scale.
sentences.quote_normalized: klinkhamer and volovik~ proposed a q-theory equilibrium-relaxation mechanism in which the cosmological-constant scale is dynamically driven to the qcd scale.

sentences.quote: Van Waerbeke and Zhitnitsky~\cite{VanWaerbeke2025} sharpened this picture into a parameter-free prediction $\rhoDE \sim \frac{\Lqcd^6}{\MP^2} \simeq (1.4\,\mathrm{meV})^4$ which sits within roughly $240\times$ of the observed $\rhoDE \approx (2.3\,\mathrm{meV})^4$.
sentences.quote_normalized: van waerbeke and zhitnitsky~ sharpened this picture into a parameter-free prediction $\rhode \sim \frac{\lqcd^6}{\mp^2} \simeq (1.4\,mev)^4$ which sits within roughly $240\times$ of the observed $\rhode \approx (2.3\,mev)^4$.
sentences.agent_notes: (1.4 meV)^4 ≈ 3.84e-12 eV^4. (2.3 meV)^4 ≈ 2.8e-11 eV^4. Ratio ≈ 7.3 NOT 240. WAIT — paper text gives different number. Cross-check: the 6.71e-9 eV^4 (also stated in abstract) vs 2.8e-11 = 240. But (1.4 meV)^4 = 1.4^4 * 1e-12 = 3.84e-12, not 6.71e-9. SLIGHT INCONSISTENCY between (1.4 meV)^4 framing and 6.71e-9 eV^4 framing in §3.

sentences.quote: Both proposals identify QCD topological structure as the common origin: the $\theta$-vacuum that suppresses the neutron EDM also generates the cosmological constant.
sentences.quote_normalized: both proposals identify qcd topological structure as the common origin: the $\theta$-vacuum that suppresses the neutron edm also generates the cosmological constant.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive summary.

sentences.quote: We encode the QCD $\theta$-angle as a Lean structure (verbatim ThetaVacuum) where the second field is a load-bearing invariant: any inhabitant of \verb|ThetaVacuum| must satisfy the Pendlebury~\textit{et al.\/}\ neutron-EDM bound~\cite{Pendlebury2015}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we encode the qcd $\theta$-angle as a lean structure (verbatim thetavacuum) where the second field is a load-bearing invariant: any inhabitant of \verb|thetavacuum| must satisfy the pendlebury~et al.\/\ neutron-edm bound~.
sentences.agent_notes: ThetaVacuum exists in Lean module StrongCPTopologicalDE (StrongCPTopologicalDE.ThetaVacuum). Pendlebury2015 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: Theorem (\texttt{theta\_planck\_natural\_violates\_edm\_bound}). Any inhabitant with $\theta = 1$ violates the structural invariant by nine orders of magnitude.
sentences.quote_normalized: theorem (theta\_planck\_natural\_violates\_edm\_bound). any inhabitant with $\theta = 1$ violates the structural invariant by nine orders of magnitude.
sentences.agent_notes: Lean: StrongCPTopologicalDE.theta_planck_natural_violates_edm_bound exists.

sentences.quote: At $\Lqcd = 0.1\,\mathrm{GeV}$ this gives $\rhoDE \approx 6.71 \times 10^{-9}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at $\lqcd = 0.1\,gev$ this gives $\rhode \approx 6.71 \times 10^{-9}\,ev^4$.
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute via formulas zhitnitskyDE_eV4(0.1)=0.1^6*6.71e-3=6.71e-9 eV^4. Matches.

sentences.quote: The observed value from joint Planck--DESI cosmological-constraint analyses is $\rhoDE \approx 2.8 \times 10^{-11}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$, so the prediction is within a factor $240\times$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the observed value from joint planck--desi cosmological-constraint analyses is $\rhode \approx 2.8 \times 10^{-11}\,ev^4$, so the prediction is within a factor $240\times$.
sentences.agent_notes: Ratio 6.71e-9 / 2.8e-11 = 239.6; rounds to 240. Matches.

sentences.quote: The formalization carries this result as a numerical anchor theorem \verb|zhitnitsky_DE_at_lambda_qcd_within_3_orders|, asserting $|\rhoDE^{\mathrm{pred}} / \rhoDE^{\mathrm{obs}} - 240| < 10^{-7}$ at the Particle-Data-Group reference scale; this is a falsifiable \verb|norm_num|-backed inequality, not a qualitative claim.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization carries this result as a numerical anchor theorem \verb|zhitnitsky_de_at_lambda_qcd_within_3_orders|, asserting $|\rhode^{pred} / \rhode^{obs} - 240| < 10^{-7}$ at the particle-data-group reference scale; this is a falsifiable \verb|norm_num|-backed inequality, not a qualitative claim.
sentences.agent_notes: Re-scan 2026-04-27T23:30:00Z: paper §3 prose now states 'asserting rho_DE^pred(Lambda_QCD = 0.1 GeV) < 1e-7 eV^4 at the Particle-Data-Group reference scale; this is a falsifiable norm_num-backed inequality bounding the prediction itself above the observed scale by no more than four orders of magnitude (the realised value 6.71e-9 eV^4 sits two orders below the asserted upper bound).' This matches the actual Lean statement `zhitnitskyDE_eV4 0.1 < 1.0e-7`. TN/SD drift resolved by post-strengthening rewrite.

sentences.quote: A companion theorem \verb|zhitnitsky_DE_far_below_planck| confirms that the prediction sits approximately $120$ orders of magnitude below the Planck-natural estimate $\MP^4 \approx 10^{112}\,\mathrm{eV}^4$, recovering the hierarchy-suppression scale that motivates the proposal.
sentences.quote_normalized: a companion theorem \verb|zhitnitsky_de_far_below_planck| confirms that the prediction sits approximately $120$ orders of magnitude below the planck-natural estimate $\mp^4 \approx 10^{112}\,ev^4$, recovering the hierarchy-suppression scale that motivates the proposal.

sentences.quote: Figure caption: Zhitnitsky topological-dark-energy prediction vs observed rho_DE. Left: log-log scan across Lambda_QCD in [1 MeV, 1 GeV]. Right: cosmological-constant hierarchy (Planck-natural M_P^4 ~ 10^112 eV^4 vs Zhitnitsky ~ 7e-9 eV^4 vs observed ~ 3e-11 eV^4) demonstrates ~120-order suppression.
sentences.quote_normalized: figure caption: zhitnitsky topological-dark-energy prediction vs observed rho_de. left: log-log scan across lambda_qcd in [1 mev, 1 gev]. right: cosmological-constant hierarchy (planck-natural m_p^4 ~ 10^112 ev^4 vs zhitnitsky ~ 7e-9 ev^4 vs observed ~ 3e-11 ev^4) demonstrates ~120-order suppression.
sentences.agent_notes: Figure file fig_zhitnitsky_de_theta_scan.png exists. Numerics consistent (7e-9 ≈ 6.71e-9, 3e-11 ≈ 2.8e-11).

sentences.quote: The bridge from the Standard-Model $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ anomaly cancellation through the strong-CP $\theta$-bound to the cosmological-constant scale ships as a three-conjunct predicate \verb|IsAnomalyMatchingCompatible| asserting (a) the SM anomaly content matches modulo $16$ in the presence of right-handed neutrinos, (b) the neutron-EDM bound on $\theta$ is satisfied, and (c) the Zhitnitsky prediction lies within the three-orders-of-magnitude observation window.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bridge from the standard-model $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ anomaly cancellation through the strong-cp $\theta$-bound to the cosmological-constant scale ships as a three-conjunct predicate \verb|isanomalymatchingcompatible| asserting (a) the sm anomaly content matches modulo $16$ in the presence of right-handed neutrinos, (b) the neutron-edm bound on $\theta$ is satisfied, and (c) the zhitnitsky prediction lies within the three-orders-of-magnitude observation window.

sentences.quote: A separate consistency check links the framing-anomaly constraint $c_- \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$ to the strong-CP bound. Modular invariance forces $\exp(2\pi i\,c_-/24) = 1$ at the Standard-Model framing-anomaly content $c_- = 24$, and the proof of this auxiliary theorem invokes the project's framing-anomaly constraint at the relevant integer point rather than restating it.
sentences.quote_normalized: a separate consistency check links the framing-anomaly constraint $c_- \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$ to the strong-cp bound. modular invariance forces $\exp(2\pi i\,c_-/24) = 1$ at the standard-model framing-anomaly content $c_- = 24$, and the proof of this auxiliary theorem invokes the project's framing-anomaly constraint at the relevant integer point rather than restating it.
sentences.agent_notes: Cross-bridge to ModularInvarianceConstraint.framing_anomaly_constraint — module exists in lean_deps.

sentences.quote: The structural punch line of the formalization is a falsification: \emph{both the Zhitnitsky topological mechanism and a residual Klinkhamer--Volovik q-theory contribution cannot be simultaneously active.}
sentences.quote_normalized: the structural punch line of the formalization is a falsification: both the zhitnitsky topological mechanism and a residual klinkhamer--volovik q-theory contribution cannot be simultaneously active.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchors the W1 correctness-push.

sentences.quote: Concretely, the theorem \verb|combined_zhitnitsky_qtheory_exceeds_observation| shows that \emph{any} positive q-theory contribution $\rho_{q} > 0$ added to the Zhitnitsky prediction at the Particle-Data-Group QCD scale exceeds the observed dark-energy density by more than a factor of three.
sentences.quote_normalized: concretely, the theorem \verb|combined_zhitnitsky_qtheory_exceeds_observation| shows that any positive q-theory contribution $\rho_{q} > 0$ added to the zhitnitsky prediction at the particle-data-group qcd scale exceeds the observed dark-energy density by more than a factor of three.
sentences.agent_notes: Theorem exists. Statement: H_BothActiveGivesInconsistency (zhitnitskyDE_eV4 0.1 + ρ_q) when ρ_q > 0. The 'factor of three' specific bound is implied: 6.71e-9 / 2.8e-11 = 240 already, plus more.

sentences.quote: The Lean module \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/StrongCPTopologicalDE.lean} ships eight substantive theorems, zero \texttt{sorry} statements, and zero new axioms beyond the Lean~4 kernel's standard \texttt{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean module lean/skefthawking/strongcptopologicalde.lean ships eight substantive theorems, zero sorry statements, and zero new axioms beyond the lean~4 kernel's standard propext, classical.choice, quot.sound.
sentences.agent_notes: IA recheck: grep '^theorem ' = 8; grep '^  sorry' = 0; grep '^axiom ' = 0. All match.

sentences.quote: A Python mirror in \texttt{src/strong\_cp\_de/} provides numerical evaluation of the Zhitnitsky prediction across $\Lqcd$, the combined-mechanism falsifier, and a $14$-case \texttt{pytest} suite cross-checking the Lean theorems against numerical agreement.
sentences.quote_normalized: a python mirror in src/strong\_cp\_de/ provides numerical evaluation of the zhitnitsky prediction across $\lqcd$, the combined-mechanism falsifier, and a $14$-case pytest suite cross-checking the lean theorems against numerical agreement.
sentences.agent_notes: IA recheck: grep '^def test' tests/test_strong_cp_de.py = 14. Matches.

sentences.quote: Treating the strong-CP problem and the cosmological-constant problem as a structurally connected bridge --- rather than two independent fine-tunings --- yields a falsifiable formal account that pins down two precise consequences.
sentences.quote_normalized: treating the strong-cp problem and the cosmological-constant problem as a structurally connected bridge --- rather than two independent fine-tunings --- yields a falsifiable formal account that pins down two precise consequences.

sentences.quote: First, the Planck-natural value $\theta = 1$ is structurally excluded by the experimental neutron-EDM bound.
sentences.quote_normalized: first, the planck-natural value $\theta = 1$ is structurally excluded by the experimental neutron-edm bound.

sentences.quote: Second, the Klinkhamer--Volovik q-theory mechanism and the Van Waerbeke--Zhitnitsky topological mechanism cannot both be active without exceeding the observed dark-energy density.
sentences.quote_normalized: second, the klinkhamer--volovik q-theory mechanism and the van waerbeke--zhitnitsky topological mechanism cannot both be active without exceeding the observed dark-energy density.

sentences.quote: The no-free-parameter Zhitnitsky prediction $\Lqcd^6/\MP^2$ matches the observed value within three orders of magnitude at the Particle-Data-Group QCD scale.
sentences.quote_normalized: the no-free-parameter zhitnitsky prediction $\lqcd^6/\mp^2$ matches the observed value within three orders of magnitude at the particle-data-group qcd scale.
sentences.agent_notes: 240x match is within 3 orders.

Paper §Intro now states 6.71e-9 eV^4 directly (replaces (1.4 meV)^4 typo).
